meta.title: Testing Strategy for Modern Web Applications
meta.excerpt: A comprehensive testing pyramid approach for Next.js applications with practical examples

props.body: A robust testing strategy is non-negotiable for production applications. Tests are not about proving your code works - they are about enabling confident changes and catching regressions before users do.

props.text: The testing pyramid guides investment in different test types. More tests at the base (fast, isolated) and fewer at the top (slow, integrated) provides the best balance of confidence and maintenance cost.
Unit tests verify individual functions and modules in isolation. They should be fast enough to run on every file save and provide immediate feedback during development.

features.title: Business Logic
features.description: Domain rules, state machines, data transformations

features.title: Utility Functions
features.description: String manipulation, date handling, type guards

features.title: Custom Hooks
features.description: State logic, side effects, data fetching hooks

props.title: Testing Boundaries
props.body: Focus unit tests on logic, not implementation details. If refactoring forces you to change tests that did not change behavior, your tests are too tightly coupled.

props.text: Integration tests verify that components work together correctly. React Testing Library encourages testing from the user perspective rather than implementation details.

idealFor: Testing what users see and do

props.text: End-to-end tests verify complete user journeys through the application. They run against a real browser and catch issues that unit and integration tests miss.
Coverage metrics guide testing investment but are not goals in themselves. High coverage with poor test quality is worse than moderate coverage with meaningful tests.

props.title: Recommended Coverage Targets
rows: Pure functions should be fully tested

props.title: Coverage Pitfalls
props.body: Do not chase 100% coverage for its own sake. Some code (error boundaries, third-party integrations) may be better tested manually. Focus coverage efforts where bugs are most costly.

features.title: Test Behavior, Not Implementation
features.description: Tests should survive refactoring if behavior stays the same

features.title: Fast Feedback Loops
features.description: Tests run frequently are tests that catch bugs early

features.title: Test at the Right Level
features.description: Unit for logic, integration for composition, E2E for journeys

features.title: Meaningful Assertions
features.description: Each test should verify something a user would care about

props.body: Test behavior, not implementation. Focus on what users experience, not internal details. A well-structured testing pyramid with meaningful assertions at each level enables confident changes and catches regressions before they reach production.
